creator: freebsd@nerdbynature.de
text:
Chapter 18.13 ("Encrypting Swap") of the FreeBSD handbook[0] mentions that geli_swap_flags can be used to alter default settings for encrypted swap devices. This flag has been made obsolete with commit r252310 [1] and during bootup (10.1-RELEASE) the following is printed now:

 /etc/rc: WARNING: $geli_swap_flags is obsolete.  Ignored.

So, if geli_swap_flags is really obsolete it should be removed from the documentation. But I wonder if it's still possible to change certain settings for encrypted swap devices.

Thanks,
C.

[0] https://www.freebsd.org/doc/handbook/swap-encrypting.html
[1] http://svnweb.freebsd.org/base?view=revision&revision=252310
